close_reason: Arc<Client> is correct: Client is shared across async tasks. Double Arc is negligible overhead.
description:
secretx-aws-sm/src/lib.rs:39 stores 'client: Arc<aws_sdk_secretsmanager::Client>'. aws_sdk_secretsmanager::Client is internally a thin wrapper around an Arc<...Handle> (the aws-sdk-secretsmanager docs explicitly state 'Client can be cheaply cloned and re-used'). Wrapping it in another Arc adds one atomic refcount layer with no shared-ownership benefit.

What goes wrong: nothing functional; pure noise. A serious Rust reader pauses to ask 'what shared mutable state is being protected here?' and finds none.

This is the opposing-pair counterpart to a forthcoming decision record (filed alongside) defending the current shape for cross-crate uniformity with secretx-aws-kms (which has the same Arc<Client> pattern; see crate-secretsx-csz6 / crate-secretsx-vfxr for the matching pair on the KMS crate).

Pick one of the two and close the other as 'won't fix'. Do not retain both.

If kept: leave a comment '// Arc<Client> is for uniformity with sister AWS backends; Client is internally Arc'd already.'
title: secretx-aws-sm: Arc<aws_sdk_secretsmanager::Client> is doubly reference-counted
